title: 2026 Golden Globes Cinematic Box Office: Ryan Coogler Wins for Sinners
text: At the 2026 Golden Globes ceremony, filmmaker Ryan Coogler accepted the cinematic and box office achievement award for the Warner Bros. vampire film 'Sinners', which earned $368.2 million globally after its April theatrical release. Coogler took the stage alongside producers Zinzi Coogler and Sev Ohanian, along with cast members. The film stars Michael B. Jordan, Hailee Steinfeld, and Jack O'Connell. During his acceptance remarks, Coogler reflected on the uncertainty filmmakers face: 'We didn't know that they would show up, so we just want to say thank you that they did. It meant the world to us.' This marked the third year for this Golden Globes category. Competing films needed to exceed $150 million at the global box office and $100 million domestically to qualify, with streaming titles eligible if viewership could be verified. Other nominees included 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' ($1.2 billion), 'Zootopia 2' ($1.6 billion), 'Mission: Impossible — The Final Reckoning' ($598.7 million), and 'Wicked: For Good' ($522.2 million).
